name: UGC NET CSE Exam | December 2012 | Paper 2

questions.text:
Consider the circuit shown below. IN a certain steady state, ‘Y’ is at logical ‘l’. What are the possible values of A, B, C?

  1. $A=0, B=0, C=1$
  2. $A=0, B=C=1$
  3. $A=1, B=C=0$
  4. $A= B=1, C=1$

The worst case time complexity of AVL is tree is better in comparison to binary search tree for

  1. Search and Insert Operations
  2. Search and Delete Operations
  3. Insert and Delete Operations
  4. Search, Insert and Delete Operations

The GSM network is divided into the following three major systems:

  1. SS, BSS, OSS
  2. BSS, BSC, MSC
  3. CELL, BSS, OSS
  4. SS, CELL, MSC

The power set of the set $\{ \Phi \}$ is

  1. $\{ \Phi \}$
  2. $\{ \Phi, \{ \Phi \} \}$
  3. $\{ 0 \}$
  4. $\{ 0, \Phi , \{ \Phi \} \}$

If the disk head is located initially at 32, find the number of disk moves required with FCFS if the disk queue of I/O blocks requests are 98. 37, 14, 124, 65, 67.

  1. 239
  2. 310
  3. 321
  4. 325

Component level design is concerned with

  1. Flow oriented analysis
  2. Class based analysis
  3. Both of the above
  4. None of the above

The ‘C’ language is

  1. Context free language
  2. Context sensitive language
  3. Regular language
  4. None of the above

The Mobile Application Protocol (MAP) typically runs on top of which protocol?

  1. SNMP (Simple Network Management Protocol)
  2. SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol)
  3. SS7 (Signalling System 7)
  4. HTTP (Hyper Text Transfer Protocol)

If a packet arrive with M-bit value is ‘1’ and a fragmentation offset value ‘0’, then it is ____ fragement.

  1. First
  2. Middle
  3. Last
  4. All of the above

The number of bit strings of length eight that will either start with a 1 bit and end with two bits 00 shall be

  1. 32
  2. 64
  3. 128
  4. 160

In compiler design ‘reducing the strength’ refers to

  1. reducing the range of values of input variables
  2. code optimization using cheaper machine instructions
  3. reducing efficiency of program
  4. none of the above

In which addressing mode, the effectives address of the operand is generated by adding a constant value to the content of a register?

  1. Absolute mode
  2. Indirect mode
  3. Immediate mode
  4. Index mode

Which of the following is true?

  1. A relation in BCNF is always in 3NF
  2. A relation in 3NF is always in BCNF
  3. BCNF and 3NF are same
  4. A relation in BCNF is not in 3NF

Given a memory partitions of 100 K, 500 K, 200 K, 300 K and 600 K (in order) and processes of 212 K, 417 K, 112 K and 426 K (in order), using the first fit algorithm, in which partition would the process requiring 426 K be placed?

  1. 500 K
  2. 200 K
  3. 300 K
  4. 600 K

What is the size of the Unicode character in Windows Operating System?

  1. 8-Bits
  2. 16-Bits
  3. 32-Bits
  4. 64-Bits

In which tree, for every node the height of its left subtree and right subtree differ almost by 1?

  1. Binary Search Tree
  2. AVL Tree
  3. Threaded Binary Tree
  4. Complete Binary Tree

The design issue of Datalink layer in OSI Reference Model is

  1. Framing
  2. Representation of bits
  3. Synchronization of bits
  4. Connection control

Given the following expressions of a grammar

$E \rightarrow E * F / F + E / F$

$F \rightarrow F – F / id$

Which of the following is true?

  1. $*$ has the higher precedence than +
  2. - has the higher precedence than $*$
  3. + and – have same precedence
  4. + has the higher precedence than $*$

The maturity levels used to measure a process are

  1. Initial, Repeatable, Defined, Managed, Optimized
  2. Primary, Secondary, Defined, Managed, Optimized
  3. Initial, Stating, Defined, Managed, Optimized
  4. None of the above

The problem of indefinite blockage of low-priority jobs in general priority scheduling algorithm can be solved using:

  1. Parity bit
  2. Aging
  3. Compaction
  4. Timer

Which API is used to draw a circle?

  1. Circle ()
  2. Ellipse ()
  3. Round Rect ()
  4. Pie ()

In DML, RECONNCT command cannot be used with

  1. OPTIONAL Set
  2. FIXED Set
  3. MANDATOR Set
  4. All of the above

Coaxial cables are categorized by Radio Government rating are adapted for specialized functions. Category RG-59 with impedance 75 $\Omega$ used for

  1. Cable TV
  2. Ethernet
  3. Thin Ethernet
  4. Thick Ethernet

RAD stands for

  1. Rapid and Design
  2. Rapid Aided Development
  3. Rapid Application Development
  4. Rapid Application Design

Suppose that someone starts with a chain letter. Each person who receives the letter is asked to send it on to $4$ other people. Some people do this, while some do not send any letter. How many people have seen the letter, including the first person, if none receives more than one letter and if the chain letter ends after there have been 100 people who read it but did not send it out? Also find out how many people sent out the letter?

  1. $122$ & $22$
  2. $111$ & $11$
  3. $133$ & $33$
  4. $144$ & $44$

A hash function $f$ defined as $f (key) = key \mod 13$, with linear probing is used to insert keys 55, 58, 68, 91, 27, 145. What will be the location of 79?

  1. 1
  2. 2
  3. 3
  4. 4

Which of the following is true while converting CFG to an LL(1) grammar?

  1. Removing left recursion alone
  2. Factoring the grammar alone
  3. Removing left recursion and factoring the grammar
  4. None of the above

Identify the Risk factors which are associated with Electronic payment system

  1. Fraudulent use of credit card
  2. Sending credit card details over internet
  3. Remote storage of credit card details
  4. All of the above

Which of the following are two special functions that are meant for handling exception, that  occur during exception handling itself?

  1. Void terminate () and void unexpected()
  2. Non void terminate () and void unexpected()
  3. Void terminate () and non void unexpected()
  4. Non void terminate () and non void unexpected()

Which of the following memory allocation scheme suffers from external fragmentation?

  1. Segmentation
  2. Pure demand paging
  3. Swapping
  4. Paging

Basis path testing falls under

  1. system testing
  2. white box testing
  3. black box testing
  4. unit testing

The User Work Area (UWA) is a set of Program variables declared in the host program to communicate the contents of individual records between

  1. DBMS and the Host record
  2. Host program and the Host record
  3. Host program and DBMS
  4. Host program and Host language

Consider the tree given below:

Using the property of eccentricity of a vertex, find every vertex that is the centre of the given tree:

  1. d & h
  2. c & k
  3. g, b, c, h, i, m
  4. c & h

The maximum number of keys stored in a B-tree of order $m$ and depth $d$ is

  1. $m^{d +1}-1$
  2. $\frac{m^{d+1}-1}{m-1}$
  3. $(m-1)(m^{d+1}-1)$
  4. $\frac{m^d-1}{m-1}$

Which of the following is the most powerful parsing method?

  1. LL(I)
  2. Canonical LR
  3. SLR
  4. LALR

In UNIX, which of the following command is used to set the task priority?

  1. init
  2. nice
  3. kill
  4. PS

AES is a round cipher based on the Rijndal Algorithm that uses a 128 – bit block of data. AES has three different configurations. ______ rounds with a key size of 128-bits, ______ rounds with a key size of 192 bits and ______ rounds with a key size of 256 –bits

  1. 5, 7, 15
  2. 10, 12, 14
  3. 5, 6, 7
  4. 20, 12, 14

Match the following $\textsf{IC}$ families with their basic circuits :

$$\begin{array}{clcl} \text{a.} & \text{TTL} & \text{i.} & \text{NAND} \\ \text{b.} & \text{ECL} & \text{ii.} & \text{NOR} \\ \text{c.} & \text{CMOS} & \text{iii.} & \text{Inverter} \\ \end{array}$$

$\textbf{Code :}$

  1. $\text{a-i, b-ii, c-iii}$
  2. $\text{a-iii, b-ii, c-i}$
  3. $\text{a-ii, b-iii, c-i}$
  4. $\text{a-ii, b-i, c-iii}$

Match the following with respect to $\text{C++}$ data types :

$$\begin{array}{clcl} \text{a.} & \text{User defined type} & \text{i.} & \text{Qualifier} \\ \text{b.} & \text{Built in type} & \text{ii.} & \text{Union} \\ \text{c.} & \text{Derived Type} & \text{iii.} & \text{Void} \\ \text{d.} & \text{Long double} & \text{iv.} & \text{Pointer} \\ \end{array}$$

$\textbf{Code :}$

  1. $\text{a-ii, b-iii, c-iv, d-i}$
  2. $\text{a-iii, b-i, c-iv, d-ii}$
  3. $\text{a-iv, b-i, c-ii, d-iii}$
  4. $\text{a-iii, b-iv, c-i, d-ii}$

Given an empty stack, after performing push(1), push(2), pop, push(3), push(4), pop, pop, push(5), pop, what is the value of the top of the stack?

  1. 4
  2. 3
  3. 2
  4. 1

Enumeration is a process of

  1. Declaring a set of numbers
  2. Sorting a list of strings
  3. Assigning a legal values possible for a variable
  4. Sequencing a list of operators

Which of the following mode declaration used in C++ to open a file for input?

  1. ios : : app
  2. in : : ios
  3. ios : : file
  4. ios : : in

Data Encryption Techniques are particularly used for _____

  1. protecting data in Data Communication System
  2. reduce Storage Space Requirement
  3. enhances Data Integrity
  4. decreases Data Integrity

Let L be set accepted by a non-deterministic finite automaton.  The number of states in non-deterministic finite automaton is $\mid Q \mid$. The maximum number of states in equivalent finite automaton that accepts L is

  1. $\mid Q \mid$
  2. $ 2 \mid Q \mid$
  3. $2^{\mid Q \mid} -1$
  4. $2^{\mid Q \mid}$

What is the result of the following expression?

(1 & 2) + (3 & 4)

  1. 1
  2. 3
  3. 2
  4. 0

Back propagation is a learning technique that adjusts weights in the neutral network by propagating weight changes.

  1. Forward from source to sink
  2. Backward from sink to source
  3. Forward from source to hidden nodes
  4. Backward from sink to hidden nodes

Match the following :

$$\begin{array}{clcl} \text{a.} & \text{TTL} & \text{i.} & \text{High fan out} \\ \text{b.} & \text{ECL} & \text{ii.} & \text{Low propagation delay} \\ \text{d.} & \text{CMOS} & \text{iii.} & \text{High power dissipation} \\ \end{array}$$

$\textbf{Code :}$

  1. $\text{a-iii, b-ii, c-i}$
  2. $\text{a-i, b-ii, c-iii}$
  3. $\text{a-i, b-iii, c-ii}$
  4. $\text{a-iii, b-i, c-ii}$

_____ is an “umbrella” activity that is applied throughout the software engineering process.

  1. Debugging
  2. Testing
  3. Designing
  4. Software quality assurance

Identify the operation which is commutative but not associative?

  1. OR
  2. NOR
  3. EX-OR
  4. NAND

Given a Relation POSITION (Posting-No, Skill), then the query to retrieve all distinct pairs of posting-nos. requiring skill is

  1. Select p.posting-No, p.posting-No from position p where p.skill=p.skill and p.posting-No < p.posting-No
  2. Select p$_1$.posting-No, p$_2$.posting-No from position p$_1$, position p$_2$  where p$_1$.skill=p$_2$.skill
  3. Select p$_1$.posting-No, p$_2$.posting-No from position p$_1$, position p$_2$  where p$_1$.skill=p$_2$.skill and p$_1$.posting.No < p$_2$.posting-No
  4. Select p$_1$.posting-No, p$_2$.posting-No from position p$_1$, position p$_2$  where p$_1$.skill=p$_2$.skill and p$_1$.posting.No = p$_2$.posting-No
